features.name: Open straight to the one next thing
features.desc: Kill the dashboard. No grid, no graphs on launch — just the single habit due right now and a button the size of your thumb. The dashboard was you showing off, not them getting it done.

features.name: A streak you can feel, not a number you forget
features.desc: One honest line that grows — a thread that gets longer, not a 47 in gray text. People don't protect a counter. They protect a thing they can see they're building.

features.name: The one-question setup
features.desc: "What's one thing you want to do every day?" That's onboarding. Done. Five screens of preferences is five chances to leave. Ask once, start now.

killed: a settings page for dark mode (just ship dark)

verdict.quote: Most of what you built was you talking to yourself — the dashboard, the badges, the chatbot. The one-tap today screen is the whole product. Cut the other nine and ship those three. That version I'd put my name on.
